text:
Tom Daley and Noah Williams were given an "absolute schooling" after earning a silver medal in the men's synchronised 10m platform at the2024 Olympicsin Paris, according to former star-turnedBBCpundit Leon Taylor.

The Team GB pair performed to a high level in the diving final asDaley collected his fifth Olympic medal. But it wasn't enough for gold as the duo finished some distance behind China's outstanding team of Junjie Lian and Hao Yang.

Taylor gave a brutal reminder to Daley, 30, and Williams, 24, of just how exceptional the Chinese divers were, claiming their "masterclass" proved to be an "absolute schooling" for the rest of the competition.

"Unbelievable. I honestly cannot tell you the masterclass, absolute schooling China have given the rest of the world," Taylor said, while working as a commentator for BBC, following the dive that claimed gold.

"That is the biggest score I have ever seen. Their dives are next level. Oh my word."

China blew away their rivals by scoring 103.23 on their final dive to take a deserved gold, while Daley and Williams were handed a huge score of 93.24 for their final dive.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Tonia Couch, a Commonwealth Games silver medallist diver, commended Daley and Williams for coping with the pressure.

"Tom Daley is actually a robot. He knows what he's doing," Couch said on BBC Sport. "The pressure on Noah must have been horrendous.

"I feel so proud of Noah and he should be so, so proud of himself. I don't think I would've been able to deal with it. He was so cool. It was truly deserved today."

It marked a surprise return to the Games for Daley, who had essentially confirmed his retirement after winning Gold at the Tokyo 2020 Olympics. And it remains to be seen whether this will be his final appearance on the global stage for Team GB.

READ MORE:Show your colours and get Team GB's official kit for the Paris Olympics



Scarlett Mew Jensen, Team GB's 3m synchro bronze medallist, defended Daley and Williams' performance and claimed the former can bow out of diving on a high if he decides to retire for good.

"That was incredible, the boys did so well and did us proud," Mew Jensen said, speaking on BBC Radio 5 Live. "For Noah [Williams] to step up like that has been incredible. To come in late like that. He's done amazing.

"If it is the end, he [Daley] has gone out with a bang. The next one is in LA, his home place. He has shown that he is just insanely talented and he can take as long as he wants out and come back and deliver that. It's just inspirational and it's incredible to be a part of the team that he is on."

title: Tom Daley and Noah Williams 'absolutely schooled' as BBC Olympics pundit speechless | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Joe Krishnan
description: Team GB divers Tom Daley and Noah Williams earned a silver medal in the men's synchronised 10m platform at the Olympic Games in Paris.
